text.title: AI-Generated Code Lint Presets Procedure
text.summary: 4-step procedure to apply AI-Generated Code Lint Presets end-to-end. Each step has named input, action, and output. Decision gates reference rules from `01-core-rules.xml`.

step.name: adopt-presets
step.input: Repo language stack + existing lint config
step.action: Copy `templates/ruff-ai-presets.toml` and `templates/eslint-ai-presets.json` into the repo; commit via PR; protect via CODEOWNERS (r1).

step.name: wire-ci-gate
step.action: Add a CI step that runs ruff + eslint against changed files; fail the build on any violation in the protected ruleset.

step.name: test-fire-on-canonical-bad-fixtures
step.input: Bad fixtures (no-op test, broad except, magic literal, console.log, debugger)
step.action: Run the lint against each canonical bad fixture; verify the rule fires; commit a `tests/lint-fixtures/` directory so the firing remains regression-tested.

step.name: monitor-rule-drift
step.action: Run `scripts/validate-ai-generated-code-lint-presets.py --self-test` weekly; block any PR that silently downgrades severity or adds `# noqa` without a documented exception.

section.title: Decision gates
section.p: Between steps, consult `content/06-decision-tree.xml` if the observed signal does not match expected output. The decision tree always terminates at a rule id from `01-core-rules.xml` or at `skip-this-methodology`.
